DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT MOTAWALA, AMIT PATEL, SUNILBHAI K
OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE PROFERRED BY DEFENDANT ON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF PEREZ, OSCAR
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-18-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AMIT MOTAWALA AND SUNILBHAI PATEL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT DID NOT MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION PER CCP 437C. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT SIMILAR VIOLENT INCIDENTS DID NOT PREVIOUSLY OCCUR AT THE PROPERTY AND THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT OWE A DUTY TO HIRE A SECURITY GUARD TO MONITOR THE PREMISES. THE COURT SUSTAINS PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO PARAGRAPH 6 OF THE DECLARATION OF MR. MOTAWALA AS LACKING FOUNDATION. WHILE HE IS AN OWNER OF THE PREMISES, HE FAILS TO CITE FACTS EXPLAINING HIS FAMILIARITY WITH THE CRIMINAL HISTORY OF THE HOTEL. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IS NOT PROBATIVE BECAUSE HE LACKS PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE REGARDING THE CRIMINAL HISTORY OF THE HOTEL. SEE VILLA V. MCFERREN, (1995) 35 CAL.APP.4TH 733, 739. DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES DO NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN ON THE SIMILAR INCIDENTS ISSUE. NONE OF THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES MAKES A SPECIFIC INQUIRY REGARDING THAT ISSUE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)
DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-10-2014 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LAURA LACARDO OF THE LAW OFFICES OF ANDREW SHIN, TEL. NO. (408) 615-1155 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RONALD F. BERESTKA JR. OF STONE & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. (925) 938-1555 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS AMIT MOTAWALA and SUNILBHAI PATEL's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant did not maintain its initial burden of production per CCP 437c. Defendant fails to show that similar violent incidents did not previously occur at the property and that defendant did not owe a duty to hire a security guard to monitor the premises. The court sustains plaintiff's objection to paragraph 6 of the declaration of Mr. Motawala as lacking foundation. While he is an owner of the premises, he fails to cite facts explaining his familiarity with the criminal history of the hotel. Plaintiff's deposition testimony is not probative because he lacks personal knowledge regarding the criminal history of the hotel. See Villa v. McFerren, (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 733, 739. Defendant's special interrogatories do not shift the burden on the similar incidents issue. None of the special interrogatories makes a specific inquiry regarding that issue. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976
